Plaintiff Bank Of America National Bank'S Order To Show Cause Why Appointment Of Receiver Should Not Be Confirmed And Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Granted
Off Calendar Jul-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Bank of America National Bank's Order to Show Cause Why Appointment of Receiver should not be confirmed and why Preliminary Injunction should not be granted. Appearances: Ron Oliner, Esq. of Duane Morris (213) 689-7469, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; James T. Graeb, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 595-4348, appearing for Defendants. Argued and the Court rules that a Receiver will be appointed no earlier than July 20, 2009. Rents are to be continued to be paid. Counsel meet and confer and review proposed Order. Both counsel approve Order (1) Appointment of Receiver; and (2) Temporary Restraining Order. Order signed in Open Court. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR # 12365

Plaintiff Bank Of America National Bank'S Order To Show Cause Why Appointment Of Receiver Should Not Be Confirmed And Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Granted
Off Calendar May-21-2009 Continued To Jun-17-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Bank of America National Bank's Order to Show Cause why appointment of Receiver should not be confirmed and why preliminary injunction should not be granted. Appearances: Ron Oliner, Esq. of Duane Morris (213) 689-7469, appearing for Plaintiff. Defense counsel was unable to appear. After hearing from Plaintiff's counsel, the Court rules as follows: Matter is continued to June 17, 2009, per agreement of parties. Plaintiff's counsel will give counsel notice of new date. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

Plaintiff Bank Of America National Bank'S Order To Show Cause Why Appointment Of Receiver Should Not Be Confirmed And Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Granted
Off Calendar Apr-27-2009 Continued To May-21-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA NATIONAL BANK'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER SHOULD NOT BE CONFIRMED AND WHY PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE GRANTED.
APPEARANCES: Ron Oliner, Esq. of Duane Morris 957-3104 for pltf & moving party; James T. Graeb, Esq. of Law Offices of James T. Graeb 595-4348 for deft & opposing party; (parties working to resolve matter).
RULING: The Court vacated its tentative ruling and per agreement of parties continued the matter to 05/21/09, as parties work to resolve matter. Defendant to tender May rents to plaintiff. TRO to remain in effect. (302/CWW/VC) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Plaintiff Bank Of America National Bank'S Order To Show Cause Why Appointment Of Receiver Should Not Be Confirmed And Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Granted
Off Calendar Apr-15-2009 Continued To Apr-27-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA NATIONAL BANK'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER SHOULD NOT BE CONFIRMED AND WHY PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE GRANTED.
APPEARANCES: Ron Oliner, Esq. of Duane Morris 957-3104 for pltf & moving party; James T. Graeb, Esq. of Law Offices of James T. Graeb 595-4348 for deft & opposing party.
RULING: The Court made a personal disclosure. No objections were made. The Court continued the matter to 04/27/09 upon request of parties. Defendant tendered to plaintiff in open Court the April rents of $41,700. In addition, defendant to pay to plaintiff $20,110.34 by 04/27/09. Defendant to provide to plaintiff proof of insurance on the subject property before 04/27/09. TRO to remain in effect. (302KOPP/VC) JUDGE: QUENTIN L. KOPP, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
